title: 10 best herbal creams for fever

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea creams helps with fever because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. The cream contains compounds that stimulate the immune system, reducing the severity and duration of a fever. It also has an antimicrobial effect, which helps prevent the spread of infection. Additionally, Echinacea purpurea cream may help ease body aches and joint pain associated with a fever, promoting overall comfort and well-being during the recovery process. Its soothing and calming effects also help reduce restlessness and discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Alkylamides: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the body's inflammatory response and alleviate symptoms associated with fever.
Cichoric acid: This polyphenolic acid has antimicrobial and antioxidant properties, which can help fight off underlying infections that may be causing the fever.
Ishwarenone: This alkylamide has immunomodulatory effects, which can help boost the immune system's ability to fight off infections and reduce the severity of fever symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Echinacea purpurea are used to make creams for fever because they contain high levels of alkaloids, glycosides, and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties.
Flowers: The flowers of Echinacea purpurea are used to make creams for fever because they contain a variety of flavonoids and phenolic acids that have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects.

Buds: The buds of Echinacea purpurea are used to make creams for fever because they contain bioactive compounds such as alkylamides and phenylethanoid glycosides that have immunomodulatory and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 2 cups of dried echinacea purpurea flowers in 4 cups of boiling water for 15 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a bowl to separate the plant material from the liquid.
Mix 1/4 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of coconut oil in a double boiler until melted.
Add 2 tablespoons of the infused liquid and 2 tablespoons of aloe vera gel to the melted mixture.
Pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool and solidify for 30 minutes to an hour.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams helps with fever because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, derived from the root of the ginger plant. The active compounds in ginger, such as gingerol and shogaol, work to reduce the body's temperature by increasing sweat production and promoting blood flow to the skin's surface. This helps to dissipate heat and relieve the discomfort associated with fever. Additionally, ginger's soothing and calming effects can also help to ease congestion and promote relaxation.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These compounds help to reduce fever by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, which contribute to the development of fever.
Shogaols: Shogaols have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to alleviate the discomfort and pain associated with fever, making it easier to manage.
Volatile oils (specifically, zingiberene and beta-bisabolol): These oils have a cooling effect on the body, helping to reduce the body temperature and alleviate the symptoms of fever.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are commonly used due to their high concentration of active compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
Roots: Roots of Zingiber officinale are used to make creams for fever because they contain bioactive compounds that help reduce inflammation and relieve pain.
Stems: Stems of ginger are also used, as they have a lower concentration of bioactive compounds compared to rhyzomes and roots, but still possess some anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 2 tablespoons of sliced zingiber officinale in 4 cups of boiling water for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the infused mixture through a cheesecloth or fine mesh into a bowl to separate solids.
Mix 1/4 cup of distilled water with 1 tablespoon of beeswax in a double boiler.
Add the infused mixture to the beeswax mixture and stir until beeswax is fully dissolved.
Pour the mixture into a clean container and let it cool and thicken for at least 2 hours.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis creams helps with fever because it contains quinine, a natural compound extracted from the bark of the Cinchona tree. Quinine has been used for centuries to treat malaria, which is often characterized by high fever. The antipyretic properties of quinine in Cinchona officinalis creams help reduce body temperature, alleviating discomfort and promoting relaxation. Additionally, the cream's soothing and cooling effects can provide relief from fever-related headaches and body aches, making it a popular natural remedy for fever management.
remedy_constituents: Quinine: It helps reduce fever by inhibiting the growth of malaria parasites, which can cause fever, and also has antipyretic properties to directly reduce body temperature.

Quinovic acid glycosides: These compounds help reduce fever by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, which contribute to the development of fever, and also have antipyretic effects.
Quinic acid: It helps with fever reduction by acting as an antipyretic agent, reducing the body's temperature, and also has anti-inflammatory properties to alleviate fever-related symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most used part of Cinchona officinalis for making creams to treat fever because they contain quinine, a natural compound with antipyretic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Barks: The barks are used due to their high content of quinine and other alkaloids, which help reduce fever and relieve pain.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes are also used for their quinine and alkaloid content, contributing to their fever-reducing and anti-inflammatory properties when used in creams.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 cinchona officinalis leaves from mature trees after the first frost to ensure optimal alkaloid content.
Dry the harvested leaves in a single layer at 35-40 degrees celsius for 2-3 days to preserve their properties.
Create a decoction by steeping 5-7 grams of dried cinchona officinalis leaves in 100ml of boiling water for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth into a clean container and discard the solids to obtain the liquid extract.
Mix 10-20ml of the liquid extract with 10-20g of a moisturizing base such as coconut oil or shea butter to create a rich cream.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus creams helps with fever because of its decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties. The active compounds in Eucalyptus globulus, such as eucalyptol, help to reduce swelling and ease congestion in the respiratory tract, making it easier to breathe. This can help to alleviate the discomfort associated with fever, such as headaches and body aches. Additionally, the cooling sensation of Eucalyptus globulus creams can help to bring down body temperature, providing relief from the heat and discomfort of a fever.
remedy_constituents: Eucalyptol: It helps with fever by acting as a natural decongestant, relieving respiratory congestion and reducing the symptoms of fever.
Cineole: It helps with fever by reducing inflammation and alleviating respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds, associated with fever.
Catechin: It helps with fever by acting as an antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent, reducing the severity of fever and its associated symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high eucalyptol content, which is a natural decongestant and anti-inflammatory that helps relieve fever and congestion.
Barks: Utilized for their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which aid in reducing fever and soothing respiratory issues.
Buds: Employed for their high eucalyptol content, which helps to alleviate fever, congestion, and other respiratory issues due to their decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Extract the eucalyptus globulus essential oil from fresh leaves using a steam distillation process for 2 hours.
Combine 20 grams of the extracted essential oil with 100 grams of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at 180 degrees fahrenheit for 30 minutes while stirring occasionally to ensure even melting.
Add 20 grams of coconut oil and 10 grams of shea butter to the mixture and stir well.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and solidify for at least 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba creams helps with fever because it contains flavonoids and terpenoids that have anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties. These compounds help to reduce inflammation and lower the body temperature, providing relief from fever. The antioxidants present in Ginkgo biloba also help to combat oxidative stress, which can contribute to fever. Additionally, the cream's soothing and calming effects can help to promote a sense of well-being and comfort, making it a popular natural remedy for fever relief.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: They help with fever by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to fever and body temperature regulation.
Terpenoids: They help with fever by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties, which can help lower body temperature and alleviate fever symptoms.
Bilobalide: It helps with fever by possessing antipyretic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce fever and alleviate associated symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and terpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help in reducing fever.
Seeds: They contain ginkgolic acid and bilobalide, which have anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties that help in reducing fever.
Barks: They contain flavonoids and terpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help in reducing fever.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1/2 cup of dried ginkgo biloba leaves, 1/4 cup of coconut oil and 2 tablespoons of beeswax.
Melt the coconut oil in a double boiler at 100°f for 10 minutes, then add the dried ginkgo biloba leaves.
Steep the ginkgo biloba leaves in the melted coconut oil for 20 minutes to infuse the oil with the herbal properties.
Strain the oil through a cheesecloth or fine mesh and discard the solids, then add the beeswax.
Whip the mixture until it thickens and reaches a creamy consistency, then pour into containers for use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia creams helps with fever because of their antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties. The active compound, cineole, is known to soothe and calm the skin, reducing heat and discomfort associated with fever. The cooling effect of the cream helps to lower body temperature, providing relief from chills and sweating. Additionally, the antibacterial properties of Melaleuca alternifolia creams help to combat underlying infections that may be causing the fever, promoting a speedy recovery and reducing the duration of illness.
remedy_constituents: Cineole: This terpene helps to reduce fever by acting as an antipyretic, which means it helps to lower the body temperature.
Linalool: This terpene has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to alleviate symptoms associated with fever, such as headaches and body aches.
Eucalyptol: This phenolic compound has expectorant properties, which can help to relieve congestion and coughs that often accompany fever, making it easier for the body to recover.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in essential oils, particularly Tea Tree Oil, which has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.
Buds: Contain high amounts of Tea Tree Oil, which helps to reduce fever and relieve associated symptoms.
Stems: The bark and leaves of the stems are sometimes used, but the primary essential oil is still obtained from the leaves.
remedy_recipe: Combine 100g of beeswax pellets with 200g of shea butter in a double boiler.
Stir 50g of coconut oil and 50g of jojoba oil into the mixture.
Add 10g of melaleuca alternifolia essential oil to the mixture and stir well.
Gradually add 20g of distilled water to the mixture and continue stirring.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it set for 30 minutes before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams helps with fever because they contain licorice root extract, which has anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties. The glycyrrhizin in licorice root is believed to reduce inflammation and lower body temperature. Additionally, the creams' soothing and moisturizing effects can help to calm the skin, reducing discomfort and promoting a sense of well-being. By addressing both the physical and emotional aspects of fever, Glycyrrhiza glabra creams can provide relief and support the body's natural recovery process.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: These constituents have anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties, helping to reduce fever by inhibiting the release of pro-inflammatory substances that contribute to fever.

Flavonoids: Glycyrrhiza glabra flavonoids, such as isoliquiritin and licoricidin, exhibit antipyretic and anti-inflammatory activities, which help to alleviate fever by modulating the body's temperature regulation mechanisms.

Glycyrrhetinic acid: This triterpenoid saponin has anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties, helping to reduce fever by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines that contribute to fever.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are primarily used due to their rich content of glycyrrhizin, a compound with anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties.
Roots: They also contain other compounds like flavonoids and triterpenoids, which contribute to their soothing and fever-reducing effects.
Roots: Additionally, the roots of Glycyrrhiza glabra are known for their ability to reduce inflammation and relieve pain, making them a key component in fever creams.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 10 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra root in a clean glass container.
Mix the dried root with 100 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler for 30 minutes at a temperature of 40 degrees celsius.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container and discard the solids.
Add 5 grams of beeswax to the mixture and heat it for another 10 minutes in the double boiler.

remedies.remedy_desc: Sambucus nigra creams helps with fever because they contain high levels of isorhamnetin, a powerful antiviral compound. Isorhamnetin works by inhibiting the replication of viruses, which in turn helps to reduce the severity and duration of fever. Additionally, Sambucus nigra creams often contain other beneficial compounds such as flavonoids and terpenes, which have anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties, further contributing to their fever-reducing effects. These natural compounds can provide relief from fever and promote overall well-being.
remedy_constituents: Isoorientin: Isoorientin has anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties, helping to reduce fever by suppressing the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines.

Sambunigrin: Sambunigrin has antipyretic and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to alleviate fever by inhibiting the production of prostaglandins, which are involved in the regulation of body temperature.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids in Sambucus nigra have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce fever by protecting against oxidative stress and inflammation caused by the infection.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Sambucus nigra flowers are commonly used to make creams for fever because they contain antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: Sambucus nigra leaves are used to make creams for fever due to their antipyretic and antiviral properties.
Barks: Sambucus nigra barks are used to make creams for fever because they contain compounds with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-20 grams of dried sambucus nigra flowers and leaves from a clean environment to avoid contamination.
Infuse 1 tablespoon of the dried plant material in 100ml of boiling water for 5-7 minutes to create a liquid extract.
Mix 1 teaspoon of beeswax with 5ml of the cooled liquid extract and 10ml of carrier oil in a small bowl.
Stir the mixture continuously over low heat for 5-10 minutes or until the beeswax is fully dissolved.
Pour the warm mixture into a sterile container and allow it to cool and set before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis creams helps with fever because they possess anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties. The cooling effect of aloe vera gel helps to reduce body temperature and soothe the skin. Its natural ingredients, such as vitamins A, C, and E, as well as beta-carotene, have antioxidant and antimicrobial properties that aid in fighting off infections. Additionally, aloe vera's ability to increase blood flow and reduce swelling can help alleviate fever-related discomfort, making it a popular natural remedy for fever relief.
remedy_constituents: Aloin: Helps reduce fever by acting as a mild purgative, promoting sweating and heat loss.
Acemannan: Assists in lowering body temperature by stimulating the immune system and promoting the production of cytokines that help fight infections.
Acetylated mannans: Contribute to reducing fever by modulating the immune response and inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high gel content, which helps to soothe and calm the skin.
Leaves: Used for their anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce fever and alleviate associated pain.
Leaves: Used for their antioxidant properties, which can help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals during fever.
remedy_recipe: Weigh out 100 grams of distilled water and 50 grams of aloe barbadensis gel in a double boiler.
Combine 50 grams of distilled water with 2 tablespoons of beeswax in a double boiler and melt.
Add 1 teaspoon of vitamin e oil and 1 teaspoon of cetyl alcohol to the melted beeswax mixture.
Gradually add the aloe barbadensis gel mixture to the beeswax mixture while continuously stirring with an immersion blender.
Pour the herbal aloe barbadensis cream into sterilized containers and allow to cool and set for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa creams helps with fever because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties. The active compound curcumin in turmeric has been shown to reduce prostaglandins, which are responsible for inducing fever. Additionally, curcumin has antioxidant properties that help to neutralize free radicals, which can contribute to the development of fever. The cream's topical application allows curcumin to penetrate deep into the skin, providing rapid relief from fever and associated symptoms, making it a popular natural remedy for soothing and calming the body.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: It helps with fever by reducing inflammation and modulating the body's immune response, thus alleviating the symptoms of fever.
Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): It helps with fever by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and enzymes, which contribute to the development of fever.
Bisdemethoxycurcumin (BDMC): It helps with fever by displaying potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to mitigate the effects of fever and promote overall health.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are commonly used due to their high concentration of curcumin, a bioactive compound with anti-inflammatory and fever-reducing properties.
Leaves: Leaves are used for their ability to promote sweating, which helps to reduce fever.
Roots: Roots are used for their warming and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to relieve fever and associated discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Gather 10g of dried curcuma longa root powder and 50g of coconut oil for the recipe.
Melt the coconut oil in a double boiler at low heat for 15 minutes.
Combine the melted coconut oil with 10g of beeswax and 5g of shea butter.
Add 10g of the curcuma longa root powder to the mixture and stir well for 5 minutes.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for fever are topical preparations that help alleviate the symptoms of fever by applying the healing properties of various herbs directly to the skin. These creams combine the extracts of fever-reducing herbs such as Echinacea purpurea, Zingiber officinale, Cinchona officinalis, Eucalyptus globulus, Ginkgo biloba, Melaleuca alternifolia, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Sambucus nigra, Aloe barbadensis, and Curcuma longa. If left untreated, fever can lead to dehydration, fatigue, and discomfort, negatively impacting daily life, work, and social activities.
